「你想花多少錢？」: 「他們搶過我父親兩次，今年冬天還想偷我們的食物。那是因為我們在城外務農。」

How much do you wish to spend?: The brigands would not dare try that in town.  The Sheriff would stop them fast.

How much do you wish to spend?: Thank you, but my Father thinks I am too young.  He would not permit it. But perhaps next year...

How much do you wish to spend?: You should be here during harvest time.  Then you would see many fruits and vegetables.
「你想花多少錢？」: 「你應該在收割季來。那時你會看到許多水果和蔬菜。」

How much do you wish to spend?: My father is Heinrich Pferdefedern.

How much do you wish to spend?: My mother has been dead for three years now.  I still miss her.

How much do you wish to spend?: I am Hilde, daughter of Heinrich Pferdefedern, the farmer.

How much do you wish to spend?: Many robbers ran up to father as he trotted back to town.  My father tried to fight, but they hurt him badly.  They then ran away to the southwest.

How much do you wish to spend?: Rutabagas keep well over the winter months.

How much do you wish to spend?: We have many kinds of fresh vegetables for sale today.  There are carrots, rutabagas, parsnips, and potatoes right from the ground at five for a silver. They are very good for you.  We also have some apples.

How much do you wish to spend?: I'm sorry, but my Father won't allow me to accept flowers from strangers.

「你想花多少錢？」: 衣服只有兩種尺寸——矮人和傢伙。

How much do you wish to spend?: The jars of beets and sauerkraut look like they were canned locally.
「你想花多少錢？」: 這些甜菜和酸菜罐子看起來像是在本地罐裝的。

How much do you wish to spend?: There are barrels of flour and salt next to the counter.  There also seem to be jars of pickled pigs' feet, linen cloth, and lots of other things in which you have very little interest.
「你想花多少錢？」: 櫃檯旁邊有桶桶的麵粉和鹽。似乎還有醃豬腳罐子、亞麻布，和很多你不感興趣的其他東西。

How much do you wish to spend?: It's the matador's red cape, recently repaired after he waved it in front of a minotaur.
「你想花多少錢？」: 這是鬥牛士的紅披風，最近才修過因為他在牛頭人面前揮舞過它。

How much do you wish to spend?: You see bottles, jugs, and canned goods more useful to a housewife than a Hero.
「你想花多少錢？」: 你看到的瓶子、罐子和罐裝食品對家庭主婦比對英雄更有用。

How much do you wish to spend?: There are some wooden poles leaning against the wall.
「你想花多少錢？」: 有些木竿靠在牆上。

How much do you wish to spend?: Wow, the latest technology in toothpicks by the bulk!  What will they think of next?
「你想花多少錢？」: 哇，批量生產的牙籤最新科技！他們接下來會想到什麼？

How much do you wish to spend?: This looks like a dry goods store, but it smells like a musty library.  The stove feels nice on such a crisp day.  Behind the counter and on the shelves, there are a variety of items for sale.  The shopkeeper appears to ignore you while he reads a book.
「你想花多少錢？」: 這看起來像雜貨店，但聞起來像霉濕的圖書館。在這個清爽的日子裡，爐火感覺很舒適。櫃檯後面和架子上有各種物品要賣。店主看起來在讀書時忽視你。

How much do you wish to spend?: Try paying the clerk for what you want to buy.
「你想花多少錢？」: 試著為你要買的東西付錢給店員。

How much do you wish to spend?: Behind the counter are weapons, armor, and such equipment as you would more often find in an "Adventurer's Shop".
「你想花多少錢？」: 櫃檯後面有武器、盔甲和你更常在「冒險者商店」找到的裝備。

How much do you wish to spend?: On the counter and on the shelves are assortments of canned goods, honey jars, sewing items, and odds and ends that people in a small town would need.
「你想花多少錢？」: 櫃檯上和架子上有罐裝食品、蜂蜜罐、縫製物品，和小鎮居民需要的各式雜物。

How much do you wish to spend?: Please don't throw things in here.  You might break a flask.
「你想花多少錢？」: 「請不要把東西扔進這裡。你可能會打破瓶子。」

How much do you wish to spend?: You should give your money to the shop owner.
「你想花多少錢？」: 你應該把錢給店主。

How much do you wish to spend?: This is the door to the back storage room.
「你想花多少錢？」: 這是通往後面儲藏室的門。
